source.title: Javna rasprava o projektu vjetroelektrane Gradina

summary.title_hr: Javna rasprava o projektu vjetroelektrane Gradina
summary.abstract_hr: Javna rasprava o projektu vjetroelektrane Gradina u općini Kupres, koju je organiziralo Federalno ministarstvo okoliša i turizma, obilježena je snažnim protivljenjem lokalne zajednice. Mještani su izrazili zabrinutost zbog utjecaja na okoliš, zdravlje ljudi, pravne valjanosti koncesija i nedovoljnih studija. Investitor i predstavnici ministarstva branili su projekt, naglašavajući usklađenost sa zakonom i potrebu za energetskom tranzicijom, dok su mještani zahtijevali cjelovitu studiju utjecaja i isticali brojne propuste i sporne točke.

speakers.role: voditelj postupka (Federalno Ministarstvo okoliša i turizma)
speakers.role_en: procedure manager (Federal Ministry of Environment and Tourism)
representative of the request developer (Zagreb Inspect)
councillor of the Municipal Council Tomislav Grad
president of the Speleological Society Mijatovi dvori

key_points: Federalno ministarstvo okoliša i turizma provodi javnu raspravu o prethodnoj procjeni utjecaja na okoliš za vjetroelektranu Gradina snage do 100 MW, s planiranih 17 turbina.

Investitor (Vegradina) i izrađivač zahtjeva (Zagreb Inspect) tvrde da su svi utjecaji na okoliš procijenjeni softverski i da su vrijednosti buke i zasjenjenja ispod graničnih, te da je projekt usklađen sa zakonom.

Lokalna zajednica izražava snažno protivljenje, navodeći da je koncesija pravno sporna, da su studije manjkave i da ne uzimaju u obzir kumulativni utjecaj s postojećim vjetroparkovima (Mesihovina, Oštrc).
Mještani svjedoče o nepodnošljivoj buci, vibracijama i zasjenjenju od postojećih vjetroparkova, te izražavaju strah za zdravlje, imovinu i prirodne resurse (krško područje, ponori, vodocrpilišta).

Postoji zabrinutost zbog blizine turbina kućama (neke unutar 500 metara), školama (škola za djecu s posebnim potrebama na 550m, osnovna škola na 700m) i nedostatka zakonske regulative o minimalnoj udaljenosti.
Zatražena je izrada pune studije utjecaja na okoliš i društvo (ESI standardi) koja bi obuhvatila sve kumulativne utjecaje i bila transparentna, s terenskim mjerenjima i nadzorom lokalne zajednice.
Ministarstvo je potvrdilo da će odluku o potrebi izrade pune studije donijeti na temelju svih prikupljenih primjedbi i komentara.
summary.title_en: Public discussion about the Gradina wind farm project
summary.abstract_en: The public discussion on the Gradina wind farm project in the municipality of Kupres, which was organized by the Federal Ministry of Environment and Tourism, was marked by strong opposition from the local community. Local residents expressed concern due to the impact on the environment, human health, the legal validity of concessions, and insufficient studies. The investor and ministry representatives defended the project, emphasizing compliance with the law and the need for energy transition, while local residents demanded a comprehensive impact study and pointed out numerous shortcomings and contentious points.
key_points_en: The Federal Ministry of Environment and Tourism is conducting a public discussion on the preliminary environmental impact assessment for the Gradina wind farm, with a capacity of up to 100 MW, with 17 planned turbines.

The investor (Vegradina) and the request developer (Zagreb Inspect) claim that all environmental impacts were assessed by software and that the values of noise and shading are below the limits, and that the project is compliant with the law.

The local community expresses strong opposition, stating that the concession is legally disputed, that the studies are deficient and that they do not take into account the cumulative impact with existing wind farms (Mesihovina, Oštrc).
Locals testify about unbearable noise, vibrations, and shadowing from existing wind farms, and express fear for health, property, and natural resources (karst area, sinkholes, water sources).

There is concern due to the proximity of turbines to houses (some within 500 meters), to schools (a school for children with special needs at 550m, a primary school at 700m) and the lack of legal regulation on minimum distance.

The development of a full environmental and social impact study (ESI standards) was requested, which would encompass all cumulative impacts and be transparent, with field measurements and local community oversight.
The Ministry has confirmed that it will make a decision on the need for a full study based on all collected remarks and comments.
mentioned_organizations_en: Federal Ministry of Environment and Tourism
Institute for the Protection of Monuments
Federal Ministry of Culture and Sport
